blocks:
Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ لِمَ تُحَرِّمُ مَآ أَحَلَّ ٱللَّهُ لَكَ ۖ تَبْتَغِى مَرْضَاتَ أَزْوَٰجِكَ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱)
O Prophet! Why do you disallow [yourself] what Allah has made lawful for you, seeking to please your wives? And Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (1)
قَدْ فَرَضَ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ تَحِلَّةَ أَيْمَـٰنِكُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ مَوْلَىٰكُمْ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِيمُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۲)
Allah has certainly made lawful for you the dissolution of your oaths, and Allah is your master and He is the All-knowing, the All-wise. (2)

Key Points

Different occasions of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) have been mentioned for this Ayah, but what is certain is that the Prophet, who had multiple wives, was sometimes afflicted by their jealous behaviors and, in order to gain their general satisfaction, would forgo his legitimate rights. Although these prohibitions were personal matters, since the Prophet has been introduced as the role model for society, others might have felt it was a religious ruling (Hukm Shar'i) and followed it. Therefore, Allah warns His Prophet against it, and in reality, makes the people understand that such actions by the Prophet were personal and should not be taken as a standard for others to follow. One of the reasons that the Qur'an was revealed by Allah is the tone of reproach (Tawbikh) in the Qur'an towards the Prophet in some verses, because no one reproaches himself in his official and permanent book. These very reprimands indicate that the Divine Revelation has not been distorted. We read another example of this in Ayah 43 of Surah At-Tawbah, where, concerning the Prophet granting permission to some comfort-seekers to leave the battlefield, He says: «عفا اللّه عنك لِمَ اذنت لهم» Let us not forget the observance of the more important (Aham) and the important (Muhimm). An oath is important, but preserving the rulings of the religion is more important. Therefore, if someone swears an oath to forbid something lawful upon himself, his oath is broken by paying expiation (Kaffarah). The measure of this expiation is mentioned in Ayah 89 of Surah Al-Ma'idah: «فكفّارته اطعام عشرة مساكين من اوسط ما تطعمون اهليكم او كسوتهم او تحرير رقبة فمن لم يجد فصيام ثلاثة ايام ذلك كفّارة ايمانكم اذا حلفتم» The Prophet's prohibition was a personal deprivation, made through an oath, and in this Ayah, the way out of it has been presented.

Messages

  1. The Prophets are under Divine training. «يا أيّها النبىّ لم تحرّم»
  2. Even the Prophet does not have the right to forbid upon himself what Allah has made lawful without reason. «يا أيّها النبىّ لِمَ تحرّم»
  3. The pleasure of Allah takes precedence over the pleasure of others. «لمَ تحرّم... تبتغى مرضات ازواجك»
  4. If a wife's demand goes beyond the Divine rulings and marital rights, it should not be fulfilled. «لِمَ تحرّم... تبتغى مرضات ازواجك» (Satisfying the spouse at any cost is not necessary.)
  5. One of the things that afflicts a person is the desire to gain the satisfaction of others at any cost. «تبتغى مرضات ازواجك»
  6. The reprimand of noble individuals must be accompanied by forgiveness and mercy. «يا أيّها النّبى لم تحرّم... و اللّه غفور رحيم»
  7. Allah both compensates for the past, «غفور رحيم», and opens the way for the future. «فرض اللّه لكم تحلة إيمانكم»
  8. Reproach and criticism must be accompanied by stating the solution and opening a way out. «لم تحرّم... تحلّة إيمانكم»
  9. There is no deadlock in Islam. (No ruling, not even an oath, can close all paths to a person.) «قد فرض اللّه لكم تحلّة إيمانكم»
  10. We are servants and must obey the Master; we must consider the lawful as lawful, even if it means breaking an oath. «هو مولاكم»
  11. The commands of Allah are knowledgeable and wise; He solves problems and unties knots. «فرض اللّه... و هو العليم الحكيم»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah At-Tahrim

This Surah has twelve Ayahs and was revealed in Medina. Its name is derived from the first Ayah, in which Allah censures the prohibition of what He has made lawful for man, even if this prohibition is a form of self-deprivation from lawful pleasures for various individual and social reasons. The initial Ayahs of the Surah concern the inappropriate behavior of some of the Prophet's wives towards him, and the subsequent Ayahs address the believers, urging them to be mindful of their families and their upbringing so that they do not fall into Hellfire. Then, it continues by referring to positive and negative role models among women, introducing two women in each dimension. He introduces the wives of Noah and Lot as negative role models, and the wife of Pharaoh and Lady Maryam (Mary) as positive role models.

Ayah

وَإِذْ أَسَرَّ ٱلنَّبِىُّ إِلَىٰ بَعْضِ أَزْوَٰجِهِۦ حَدِيثًا فَلَمَّا نَبَّأَتْ بِهِۦ وَأَظْهَرَهُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ عَرَّفَ بَعْضَهُۥ وَأَعْرَضَ عَنۢ بَعْضٍ ۖ فَلَمَّا نَبَّأَهَا بِهِۦ قَالَتْ مَنْ أَنۢبَأَكَ هَـٰذَا ۖ قَالَ نَبَّأَنِىَ ٱلْعَلِيمُ ٱلْخَبِيرُ (۳)
When the Prophet confided a matter to one of his wives, but when she divulged it [instead of guarding the secret] and Allah apprised him about it, he acquainted [her] with part of the matter and ignored part of it. So when he told her about it, she said, ‘Who informed you about it?’ He said, ‘The All-knowing and the All-aware has informed me.’ (3)

Messages

1- A wife must be trustworthy with secrets so that her husband can confide his secrets in her. «و اذ أسرّ النّبىّ الى بعض ازواجه»

2- It is not necessary to disclose certain matters to all members of the family. «الى بعض ازواجه»

3- Preserve the honor (reputation) of those who err, and do not mention their names. «بعض ازواجه... نبّأت به»

4- The wives of the Prophet were not infallible (ma'sum), because they did not keep His secrets. «اسرّ النّبى... نبّأت»

5- The Prophet of Islam was also involved in family issues. «اسرّ النّبى... نبّأت به»

6- Allah has special favor upon His Prophet and exposes those who are not loyal to the Prophet. «نَبّأت به... اظهره اللّه»

7- The leader of the community should not be unaware of his internal and family matters. «اظهره اللّه علیه»

8- Allah informs His Prophet through the unseen (ghayb). «اظهره اللّه علیه»

9- Mention the errors to the individuals themselves, not to others. «عرّف بعضه»

10- In management, sometimes overlooking (taghāful) and broad-mindedness (sa'at-e sadr) are necessary. «و اعرض عن بعض»

11- Do not hold all the errors of those who err against them. «و اعرض عن بعض»

12- Whoever secretly reveals the secrets of others should expect that his own secret may also be revealed. «نبّأت به... نبّأها به»

13- The source of the knowledge of the Prophets is Divine knowledge. «نبّأنى العلیم الخبیر»

14- The source of the news and the informant must be All-Knowing (Alim) and All-Aware (Khabir). «نبّأنى العلیم الخبیر» (News is valuable and reliable only if it is based on knowledge and awareness.)

15- Allah's exposure (of secrets) is a sign of His presence and supervision, and faith in the presence of the All-Knowing (Alim) and All-Aware (Khabir) God can restrain a person from any sin or conspiracy. «العلیم الخبیر»

Ayah

إِن تَتُوبَآ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ فَقَدْ صَغَتْ قُلُوبُكُمَا ۖ وَإِن تَظَـٰهَرَا عَلَيْهِ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ مَوْلَىٰهُ وَجِبْرِيلُ وَصَـٰلِحُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۖ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ ظَهِيرٌ (٤)
If the two of you repent to Allah... for your hearts have certainly swerved, and if you back each other against him, then [know that] Allah is indeed his guardian, and his supporters are Gabriel, the righteous among the faithful and, thereafter, the angels. (4)

Key Points

Ṣaghat (صغت) derives from ṣaghw (صغو), meaning inclination toward deviation. Iṣghā’ (اصغاء) refers to listening where the listener has an inclination toward the speaker. By consensus among Shia and Sunni exegetes, the two women addressed in this Ayah (تتوبا... صغت قلوبكما) concerning repentance are Hafsah and 'A'ishah, the former being the divulger of the Prophet’s secret and the latter the listener. 52 narrations from both Shia and Sunni sources state that the meaning of Ṣāliḥ al-Mu’minīn (صالح المؤمنین) is ‘Alī ibn Abī Ṭālib علیهما السلام. Based on these narrations, ‘Alī ibn Abī Ṭālib علیهما السلام is placed alongside Gabriel, or rather, between him and the rest of the angels, and the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله gave him glad tidings of this status.(1)

1) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq.

Messages

1- Do not question everyone because of a single transgression. (تتوبا) (Two people must repent, not everyone.)

2- Keep the path of return and repentance open for those who are ashamed. (ان تتوبا) Of course, every sin requires repentance.

3- Listening to a secret is also forbidden. Allah tells both the speaker and the listener to repent. (تتوبا)

4- Allah is the Master (Mawlā) of everyone and also the Master of the Prophet personally. In the second Ayah, He said: و اللّه مولاكم (and Allah is your Master), and in this Ayah, specifically regarding the Prophet, He says: هو مولاه (He is his Master).

5- Highlight the names of the distinguished figures. Although Gabriel is one of the angels, his name is mentioned separately. (جبریل... و الملائكة)

6- Sometimes a single incident or spark indicates a whole movement. Divulging a secret was minor, but Allah considered it a conspiracy. (تظاهرا علیه)

7- Being a spouse or companion is not a sign of shared thought or sympathy. (ازواجه... تظاهرا علیه)

8- Repentance is not merely expressing regret verbally; true repentance is the return of the sinner's heart. (تتوبا... صغت قلوبكما)

9- More important than deviation in action is the deviation of the spirit and heart. First, a person's thought and heart deviate, and then they commit the transgression. (صغت قلوبكما)

10- Those who divulge a secret without reason, if they do not repent, are conspirators. (ان تتوبا... تظاهرا علیه)

11- In the face of conspiracy against the Prophet and the faith, all forces must be mobilized. (تظاهرا علیه... هو مولاه و صالح المؤمنین و الملائكة... ظهیر)

12- The Truth (Haqq) is never left alone. (هو مولاه و جبریل و صالح المؤمنین و...)

13- Being righteous (Ṣāliḥ) places a person alongside the near angels of Allah. (جبریل و صالح المؤمنین)

14- The angels are helpers of the Prophet against conspiracies. (و الملائكة بعد ذلك ظهیر)

15- Divine assistance is comprehensive, both apparent and unseen. (صالح المؤمنین و الملائكة)

Ayah

عَسَىٰ رَبُّهُۥٓ إِن طَلَّقَكُنَّ أَن يُبْدِلَهُۥٓ أَزْوَٰجًا خَيْرًا مِّنكُنَّ مُسْلِمَـٰتٍ مُّؤْمِنَـٰتٍ قَـٰنِتَـٰتٍ تَـٰٓئِبَـٰتٍ عَـٰبِدَٰتٍ سَـٰٓئِحَـٰتٍ ثَيِّبَـٰتٍ وَأَبْكَارًا (٥)
It may be that if he divorces you his Lord will give him, in [your] stead, wives better than you: [such as are] muslim, faithful, obedient, penitent, devout and given to fasting, virgins and non-virgins. (5)

Key Points

Qunūt (obedience) is obedience accompanied by humility. Sā’ihāt (those who fast/travel) derives from siyāhah (travel/journey), meaning those who undertake migration (Hijrah) and fasting. Although the Prophet had multiple wives, and some of them caused him distress, the Prophet did not divorce any of them. Therefore, raising the issue of divorce in this Ayah signifies the Prophet's independence from anyone or anything, so that the Prophet's close relatives do not assume that His Holiness needs them or that they are important individuals. Rather, there may be people better and more worthy than them who aspire to be the Prophet's wife. This Ayah also demonstrates the eloquence (faṣāḥah), rhetoric (balāghah), and miraculous nature (i'jāz) of the Quran, because faith, humility, repentance, worship, and fasting can be combined in one person; hence, they were listed without the conjunction wāw (and). However, being a virgin and being a widow cannot be combined in one person; hence, the conjunction wāw (و) is placed between these two attributes. (2) The Prophet's wife must be compatible with the Prophet. In describing the characteristics, there is no mention of appearance, tribe, wealth, or fame, but faith and worship are mentioned, because spiritual perfections are appropriate for divine leadership. Marriage to a widow is mentioned alongside a virgin so that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله is not accused of merely seeking sexual gratification.

(2) Tafsir Kashshāf.

Messages

1- Man needs a spouse, and in case of divorce, he can replace her. «ان طلّقكنّ... یبدله»

2- Opposing the husband, failing to apologize, and showing no remorse may lead the wife to the brink of divorce and separation. «تظاهرا... طلّقكنّ»

3- One of the strongest levers against the conspiracy of wives is the threat of divorce. «تظاهرا علیه... طلّقكنّ»

4- Not all of the Prophet's wives were the best women of their time. «خیراً منكنّ»

5- Break the false pride of individuals and stand against vain assumptions. «خیراً منكن»

6- The true worth of a woman lies in her perfections. «خیراً منكنّ مسلمات، مؤمنات...»

7- Belief precedes action. First Islam and faith, then worship and fasting. «مسلمات مؤمنات... عابدات سائحات»

8- A woman's faith and worship are more important than whether she is a virgin or a widow. «مؤمنات... ثیّبات و ابكارا»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ قُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُمْ وَأَهْلِيكُمْ نَارًا وَقُودُهَا ٱلنَّاسُ وَٱلْحِجَارَةُ عَلَيْهَا مَلَـٰٓئِكَةٌ غِلَاظٌ شِدَادٌ لَّا يَعْصُونَ ٱللَّهَ مَآ أَمَرَهُمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ مَا يُؤْمَرُونَ (٦)
O you who have faith! Save yourselves and your families from a Fire whose fuel will be people and stones, over which are [assigned] severe and mighty angels, who do not disobey whatever Allah commands them and carry out what they are commanded. (6)

Key Points

Waqūd (وَقُود) means the substance that causes ignition, such as oil and coal. Therefore, some commentators have considered the meaning of Al-Hijārah (الحجارة) to be things like coal. We read in a hadith: The fire-starting stone is sulfur and brimstone. (3) It is narrated in traditions that upon hearing this ayah, a young man fell to the ground, and the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله promised him Paradise and said: "That is for him who fears My station and fears My threat." (4) Paradise is specifically for those who are mindful of My station and fear My promises of punishment. We read in traditions that the method of protecting relatives from the fire of Hell is enjoining them to good and forbidding them from evil. If they accept, you have protected them from the fire, and if they refuse, you have fulfilled your duty. "If they obey you, you would have protected them, and if they disobey you, you have fulfilled what was incumbent upon you." (5) We read in a narration: "May Allah have mercy upon a man who says to his family: O my family! [Mind] your prayers, your fasting, your zakat, your poor, your orphans, your neighbors." (6) The mercy of Allah be upon the one who warns his family to be mindful of their prayer, fasting, zakat, the poor, the orphans, and their neighbors. Perhaps "whose fuel is men" (وقودها النّاس) refers to the embodiment of man's deeds on the Day of Judgment, where his ugly traits and actions in this world become the kindling on that Day.

Man's Responsibility towards the Family Several verses in the Quran refer to man's mission regarding his family, including: "Protect yourselves and your families from a Fire." (7) "And enjoin prayer upon your family and be steadfast therein." (8) Enjoin your family to prayer and be persistent in it. "And warn your nearest kindred." (9) Warn your closest relatives. "O my son! Establish prayer and enjoin what is right." (10) My son! Establish prayer and enjoin good. "O Prophet! Tell your wives and your daughters and the women of the believers..." (11) O Prophet! Tell your wives and daughters, and then the women of the believers... "Indeed, the losers are those who will lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection." (12) The true losers are those who have lost themselves and their families on the Day of Judgment. "Indeed, we were previously among our families, fearing [the punishment]." (13) Men of God are concerned about their families and are not indifferent. "And he used to enjoin on his family prayer." (14) The Prophet always enjoined his family to prayer.

This subject has also received great attention in traditions, a few examples of which are mentioned: Imam Ali عليه السلام says: "Teach yourselves and your families goodness and discipline them." (15) Teach yourselves and your families goodness and discipline them. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: "All of you are shepherds, and all of you are responsible for your flock... The man is a shepherd over his household... and the woman is a shepherdess over the household of her husband and his child." (16) All of you are responsible for those under your care. The man is responsible for his family; the woman is responsible for her husband and child. The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله says: "Command your children to pray when they reach seven years of age, and strike them for abandoning it when they reach nine, and separate them in their beds when they reach ten." (17) As soon as your child reaches seven years of age, command them to pray, and when they reach nine, strictness up to the point of physical discipline is permissible, and separate their beds at the age of ten.

In Surah Al-Muddaththir, the Quran states the number of angels of Hell is nineteen: "Over it are nineteen." (18) And perhaps the description ghilāẓun shidād (غلاظ شداد - stern and severe) in this ayah (6 of At-Tahrim) refers to the characteristics of those same nineteen angels. Perhaps the meaning of "they do not disobey Allah in what He commands them" (لا یعصون اللّه ما امرهم) is the command of Allah regarding seizing, binding, and casting the inhabitants of Hell into the Fire, as we read elsewhere: "Seize him and shackle him, then into the Hellfire cast him." (19) Or: "Seize him and drag him into the midst of the Hellfire. Then pour over his head the torment of scalding water." (20) Since there are also hierarchies among the angels, "And there is none of us but has a known station" (21), perhaps the meaning of "and they do what they are commanded" (یفعلون ما یُؤمرون) refers to the commands that superior angels give to the angels under their authority, and they obey without question. Both protect yourselves, (qū anfusakum - قوا انفسكم), and ask Allah to protect you. "Our Lord... protect us from the punishment of the Fire." (22)

3) Bihar, vol. 17, p. 364. 4) Ibrahim, 14. 5) Tafsir Noor Ath-Thaqalayn. 6) Tafsir Maraghi. 7) At-Tahrim, 6. 8) Taha, 132. 9) Ash-Shu'ara, 214. 10) Luqman, 17. 11) Al-Ahzab, 59. 12) Az-Zumar, 15. 13) At-Tur, 26. 14) Maryam, 55. 15) Tafsir Maraghi. 16) Majmu'ah Warram, vol. 1, p. 6. 17) Bihar, vol. 85, p. 134. 18) Al-Muddaththir, 30. 19) Al-Haqqah, 30 - 31. 20) Ad-Dukhan, 47 - 48. 21) As-Saffat, 164. 22) Al-Baqarah, 201.

Messages

1- Having faith is not enough; preserving it from dangers and harms is important. Therefore, vigilance is necessary. "O you who have believed, protect yourselves" (یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا قوا انفسكم).

2- The first step in reform is self-reform and the reform of relatives, followed by the reform of society. In this ayah, self-reform and the reform of relatives are mentioned, and then in the ninth ayah, the reform of society through Jihad against the disbelievers and hypocrites is mentioned. "Protect yourselves and your families" (قوا انفسكم و اهلیكم).

3- Belief in the Resurrection and Hell plays a crucial role in establishing piety and reforming oneself and others. "Protect yourselves and your families from a Fire whose fuel is men and stones" (قوا انفسكم و اهلیكم نارا وقودها الناس و الحجارة).

4- The religious upbringing of children is the responsibility of the head of the family. "Protect yourselves and your families from a Fire" (قوا انفسكم و اهلیكم ناراً).

5- The human soul is rebellious and needs protection. "Protect yourselves" (قوا انفسكم).

6- The person most entitled to protect a human being is the human being himself. "Protect yourselves" (قوا انفسكم).

7- Unless we remove the chains from our own feet, we cannot free others. Self-building is a prerequisite for success in building the family and society. "Protect yourselves and your families" (قوا انفسكم و اهلیكم).

8- The punishment for abandoning one's family and relatives is becoming the fuel for the fire of Hell. "Whose fuel is men and stones" (وقودها الناس و الحجارة).

9- Those who are hard-hearted in this world, "so they are like stones or even harder in hardness" (23), will also be placed alongside stones on that Day. "Whose fuel is men and stones" (وقودها الناس و الحجارة).

10- In management, the person responsible for a task must be compatible with that task. If the guardians of Hell were not ghilāẓun shidād (stern and severe), they would either be harmed themselves or fail to properly execute their mission. "Over it are angels, stern and severe" (علیها ملائكة غلاظ شداد).

11- Although the sinful person goes to Hell, the angels of Hell will not disobey Allah in punishing him. "They do not disobey Allah in what He commands them" (لا یعصون اللّه ما امرهم).

12- The angels neither rebelled against the commands given to them in the past ("what He commands them" - ما امرهم) nor against the commands that will be given to them later ("what they are commanded" - ما یؤمرون).

23) Al-Baqarah, 74.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَا تَعْتَذِرُوا۟ ٱلْيَوْمَ ۖ إِنَّمَا تُجْزَوْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۷)
[They will call out to the faithless:] ‘O faithless ones! Do not make any excuses today. You are being requited only for what you used to do.’ (7)

Key Points

Throughout the entire Quran, only this single Ayah begins with the phrase "یا ایّها الّذین كفروا" (O you who disbelieve), addressing the disbelievers, and it pertains to the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah). The Day of Resurrection has various stations (Mawāqif): In one station of Qiyamah, they will not be permitted to apologize. "لا یؤذن لهم فیعتذرون" (24) And in other stations where they do apologize, their apologies will not be accepted. "لا ینفع الّذین ظلموا معذرتهم" (25) And in yet another station, they will not even be permitted to speak. (26)

Examples of apologies on the Day of Resurrection:

Sometimes they swear falsely: "و اللّه ربّنا ما كنا مشركین" (27) By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists. Yes, either because lying has become inherent to their nature, or because they imagine they can be saved on that Day, they swear a false oath. But in any case, their statement contains a type of apology and regret.

Sometimes they blame others: "ربّنا هؤلاء اضلّونا" (28) Our Lord! (We did not want to go astray) Our predecessors led us astray.

Sometimes they offer the excuse of obeying their leaders and predecessors: "انّا اطعنا سادتنا و كبرائنا" (29) Our Lord! We were afflicted because we followed our masters and great ones.

Sometimes they offer the excuse of their own inattention and lack of thought: "لو كنّا نسمع او نعقل..." (30) If we had listened to the call of the prophets or used our intellect, we would not have become wretched.

Sometimes they promise regret and compensation for the past: "ربّ ارجعون لعلّى اعمل صالحاً فیما تركت" (31) My Lord! Send me back to the world, so that I may perform righteous deeds in what I neglected.

24) Al-Mursalat, 36. 25) Ar-Rum, 57. 26) Ya Sin, 65. 27) Al-An'am, 23. 28) Al-A'raf, 38. 29) Al-Ahzab, 67. 30) Al-Kahf, 10. 31) Al-Mu'minun, 99 and 100.

Messages

1- Disbelief (Kufr) is unforgivable on the Day of Resurrection, and the repentance of the disbeliever on that Day is fruitless. Indeed, obstinacy, stubbornness, and fanaticism in the face of logic, admonition, and miracles close the door to any excuse. "یا ایّها الّذین كفروا لا تعتذروا الیوم"

2- The punishments of the Day of Resurrection are nothing but the consequence of man's own actions. "انّما تجزون ما كنتم تعملون"

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ تَوْبَةً نَّصُوحًا عَسَىٰ رَبُّكُمْ أَن يُكَفِّرَ عَنكُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِكُمْ وَيُدْخِلَكُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ يَوْمَ لَا يُخْزِى ٱللَّهُ ٱلنَّبِىَّ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ ۖ نُورُهُمْ يَسْعَىٰ بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَبِأَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَآ أَتْمِمْ لَنَا نُورَنَا وَٱغْفِرْ لَنَآ ۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۸)
O you who have faith! Repent to Allah with sincere repentance! Maybe your Lord will absolve you of your misdeeds and admit you into gardens with streams running in them, on the day when Allah will not let down the Prophet and the faithful who are with him. Their light will move swiftly before them and on their right. They will say, ‘Our Lord! Perfect our light for us, and forgive us! Indeed You have power over all things.’ (8)

Key Points

"Nasūh" (نَصوح) derives from "Nusḥ" (نُصح), meaning sincerity and truthfulness, and sometimes it also means firmness. In the commentaries (tafāsīr), several elements have been mentioned for Tawbah Nasūḥ (sincere repentance), such as: regret, seeking forgiveness (istighfār), abandoning sin and resolving to abandon it in the future, fear of non-acceptance, seeing the sin before oneself and feeling ashamed, weeping, speaking little, eating little, sleeping little, paying the rights of the people, and so on... It is better to refer to the narrations (Riwāyāt). If we become arrogant and say that our repentance will be accepted one hundred percent, we have paved the way for committing or repeating the transgression. Therefore, while being hopeful, we should not be one hundred percent certain. As He says in this Ayah: «عَسى ربّكم ان یكفّر عنكم سیّئاتكم» (Perhaps your Lord will remove your evil deeds from you), and in Ayah 31 of Sūrat An-Nūr, He says: «توبوا الى اللّه جمعیا ایّها المؤمنون لعلكم تفلحون» (Turn to Allah in repentance, all of you, O believers, that you might succeed). Repent, perhaps you will succeed. The word la‘alla (لَعلَّ) is a sign of hope. In the previous Ayahs, the discussion was about fire (nār); in this Ayah, the discussion is about light (nūr). In the previous Ayahs, the discussion was about prevention, «قوا انفسكم و اهلیكم نارا» (Protect yourselves and your families from a Fire); in this Ayah, the discussion is about cure, that if you could not protect yourselves and fell into sin, the path of repentance is open, and your hope should not turn into despair. «توبوا الى اللّه... عَسى ربّكم» In this world and the Hereafter, the Prophet and the believers are honored (‘azīz): In this world: «و للّه العزّة و لرسوله و للمؤمنین»(32) (And to Allah belongs [all] honor, and to His Messenger, and to the believers). Honor belongs exclusively to Allah, His Messenger, and the believers. In the Hereafter: «یوم لا یخزى اللّه النّبىّ و الّذین آمنوا معه» (The Day when Allah will not disgrace the Prophet and those who believed with him). A Day when Allah will not humiliate the Prophet and his companions. (Rather, humiliation and disgrace are reserved for the disbelievers.) The companions of the Prophet on the Day of Judgment, «آمنوا معه» (those who believed with him), are the very ones who struggled (jāhadū) with their wealth and their lives in this world, and were severe against the disbelievers and merciful among the believers. «و الّذین آمنوا معه جاهدوا باموالهم و انفسهم»(33), «و الّذین معه اشداء على الكفّار رحماء بینهم»(34) Supplication (Du‘ā) and request from Allah always play a role: whether in this world: «اهدنا الصراط المستقیم» (Guide us to the straight path), or in the Hereafter: «اتمم لنا نورنا» (Perfect for us our light). Seeking forgiveness is both in this world: «ربّنا فاغفرلنا ذنوبنا»(35) (Our Lord, forgive us our sins), and in the Hereafter: «ربّنا... واغفرلنا» (Our Lord... and forgive us).

32) Al-Munāfiqūn, 8. 33) At-Tawbah, 88. 34) Al-Fatḥ, 29. 35) Āl ‘Imrān, 193.

Messages

1- In the previous Ayahs, He commanded protection from the Fire, «قوا انفسكم...» In this Ayah, He considers sincere repentance as one of the ways to achieve that. «توبوا الى اللّه»

2- Sometimes a believer commits a sin, and he must repent. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا توبوا...»

3- Repentance must be to the threshold of Allah, not the disclosure of sin to His servants. «توبوا الى اللّه»

4- One of the duties of a believer is repentance from sin. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا توبوا...»

5- Repentance must be sincere and truthful, not merely lip service. «توبةً نصوحا»

6- Repentance has no specific time or place. «توبوا الى اللّه توبةً نصوحا»

7- To encourage repentance, people must be made hopeful of Divine Mercy. «عسى ربّكم»

8- The acceptance of repentance is among the affairs of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «عسى ربّكم ان یكفّر عنكم سیئاتكم»

9- Impurity and filth have no place in Paradise; first purification, then entry into Paradise. «یكفّر... یدخلكم»

10- The result of repentance is twofold: erasing the past «یكفّر عنكم سیئاتكم» and securing the future. «یدخلكم جنّات»

11- Belief in the Prophet is not enough; companionship and obedience to him are necessary. «و الّذین آمنوا معه»

12- Righteous deeds in this world appear as light in the Hereafter. «نورهم یسعى بین ایدیهم»

13- Those who seek light in this world, «و اتبعوا النّور الّذى انزل معه»(36) (and followed the light which was sent down with him), will be immersed in light in the Hereafter. «نورهم یسعى بین ایدیهم و من ایمانهم»

14- There is also development (perfection) in the Hereafter. Believers on that Day are concerned with perfecting their light. «ربّنا اتمم لنا نورنا»

15- True believers are concerned with the removal and reduction of evils, «و اغفر لنا» (and forgive us), and the growth and surge of good deeds. «اتمم لنا نورنا»

16- Hope for the acceptance of supplication, «اتمم لنا نورنا و اغفر لنا» (Perfect for us our light and forgive us), stems from His absolute power. «انك على كلّ شى ء قدیر»

36) Al-A‘rāf, 157.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ جَـٰهِدِ ٱلْكُفَّارَ وَٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ وَٱغْلُظْ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَمَأْوَىٰهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۹)
O Prophet! Wage jihad against the faithless and the hypocrites, and be severe with them. Their refuge will be hell, and it is an evil destination. (9)

Key Points

Jihad (struggle) against the disbelievers (kuffar) and the hypocrites (munafiqin) is a command from Allah, but its form and method are at the discretion of the Prophet and his rightful successors. Therefore, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله did not mobilize an army against the hypocrites, but during the time of Imam Ali عليه السلام, since they had formed troops and an army, that Imam fought against them.

Messages

1- War and Jihad must be under the supervision of a Divine leader. «یا ایّها النّبى جاهد الكفّار»

2- The reality of religion is heartfelt belief; therefore, those who make false declarations (who are the hypocrites) are mentioned alongside the disbelievers. «الكفّار و المنافقین»

3- Islam is a comprehensive religion. It shows compassion when appropriate, and strictness and severity when appropriate. «و اغلظ علیهم»

4- Due to disbelief (kufr) and hypocrisy (nifaq), a person reaches a point where the Prophet of Mercy must roar against him and deal with him severely. «و اغلظ علیهم»

5- The killing of disbelievers and hypocrites by the hands of the believers does not diminish their punishment in the Hereafter. «جاهد... و مأواهم جهنّم»

Ayah

ضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًا لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱمْرَأَتَ نُوحٍ وَٱمْرَأَتَ لُوطٍ ۖ كَانَتَا تَحْتَ عَبْدَيْنِ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا صَـٰلِحَيْنِ فَخَانَتَاهُمَا فَلَمْ يُغْنِيَا عَنْهُمَا مِنَ ٱللَّهِ شَيْـًٔا وَقِيلَ ٱدْخُلَا ٱلنَّارَ مَعَ ٱلدَّٰخِلِينَ (۱٠)
Allah cites an example of the faithless: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. They were under two of our righteous servants, yet they betrayed them. So they did not avail them in any way against Allah, and it was said [to them], ‘Enter the Fire, along with those who enter [it].’ (10)

Key Points

This Ayah introduces two women as prime examples of disbelief (kufr), both of whom lived in the household of prophethood and were wives of two Divine Prophets, yet they were in agreement and collaboration with their opponents. In the subsequent verses, He introduces two women as superior examples of faith: one, the wife of the polytheist and oppressor Pharaoh, and the other, Lady Maryam (Mary). At the beginning of the Surah, the discussion concerned the deviation of two of the Prophet's wives, "صغت قلوبكما" (your hearts have inclined), and He stated: If you conspire against the Prophet, Allah, the angels, and the believers are His supporters. Towards the end of this Surah, the discussion concerns the two hell-bound wives of Prophets Nuh (Noah) and Lut (Lot), who became hell-bound due to betrayal, and those two Prophets, despite their status of prophethood, cannot avert anything of Allah's wrath from their wives. Being the wife of a Prophet is not a cause for salvation. Just as being the son of a Prophet is not, rather it is a person's own performance that leads to happiness or wretchedness. In the Quran, besides this Ayah, the destruction of Lut's wife has been mentioned several times. "كانت من الغابرین" (She was among those who remained behind) (37). The best method in education is indirect admonition. Although Abu Lahab and Nuh's son were also examples of disbelief, Allah gave the example of the wives of the two Prophets and introduced the word 'betrayal' (khiyanah) so that it would be consistent with the divulging of the Prophet's صلى الله عليه وآله secret by his wife, which is a type of betrayal, and serve as a warning and caution to the Prophet's wives not to assume that affiliation with the Prophet is the key to salvation. Protecting the family from the Fire of Hell, which we read in the sixth Ayah, "قوا انفسكم و اهلیكم" (Protect yourselves and your families), does not mean 100% success for the person. Because sometimes his family members do not comply. "فخانتاهما" (So they betrayed them). We read in the narrations (Ahadith) that the wives of Nuh and Lut did not commit betrayal in the sense of sexual deviation (38); rather, the intent of the betrayal was opposition to the Prophets' programs and goals and cooperation with their opponents.

37) A'raf, 83; Hijr, 60; Shu'ara, 171; Naml, 57; Ankabut, 32 and 33; Saffat 135. 38) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 11, p. 307.

Messages

1- A woman can be a model of corruption or righteousness throughout history. "ضرب اللّه مثلاً... امرأة نوح و امرأة لوط" (Allah presents an example... the wife of Nuh and the wife of Lut).

2- One can learn manners from the unmannerly. "ضرب اللّه مثلاً... امرئة نوح" (Allah presents an example... the wife of Nuh).

3- A woman is free in belief and action. Family, economic, and social dependence do not compel her. "كانتا تحت عبدین من عبادنا... فخانتاهما" (They were under two servants among Our servants... so they betrayed them).

4- Servitude ('ubudiyyah) and righteousness were the secret to attaining the rank of prophethood. "عبدین من عبادنا صالحین" (two servants among Our righteous servants).

5- The current of disbelief (kufr) and hypocrisy penetrates even the homes of the Prophets. "فخانتاهما" (So they betrayed them).

6- Servitude and worship are valuable only when accompanied by righteous deeds. "عبدین... صالحین" (two servants... righteous).

7- In the religious family system, guardianship and management rest with the man. "كانتا تحت عبدین" (They were under two servants).

8- Corrupt affiliates do not harm the infallibility ('ismah) and justice of the heavenly leaders. "عبدین من عبادنا... فخانتاهما" (two servants among Our servants... so they betrayed them).

9- Betrayal of the school of thought (maktab) leads a person to a point where even the intercession of the Prophets is useless. "فلم یغنیا عنهما من اللّه شیئاً" (So they availed them nothing against Allah).

10- No factor prevents Divine wrath. "فلم یغنیا..." (So they availed them nothing...).

11- Betrayal is the path to Hell. "فخانتاهما... ادخلا النار" (So they betrayed them... Enter the Fire).

Ayah

وَضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًا لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱمْرَأَتَ فِرْعَوْنَ إِذْ قَالَتْ رَبِّ ٱبْنِ لِى عِندَكَ بَيْتًا فِى ٱلْجَنَّةِ وَنَجِّنِى مِن فِرْعَوْنَ وَعَمَلِهِۦ وَنَجِّنِى مِنَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱۱)
Allah cites an example of the faithful: the wife of Pharaoh, when she said, ‘My Lord! Build me a home near You in paradise, and deliver me from Pharaoh and his conduct, and deliver me from the wrongdoing lot.’ (11)

Key Points

In the previous verses, two examples of bad women (the wives of Noah and Lot) were introduced, and in verses 11 and 12, two examples of good women are introduced (the wife of Pharaoh and Lady Maryam).

When Asiyah (Pharaoh's wife) saw the miracle of Prophet Moses, she believed in him. In punishment for this act, Pharaoh nailed her hands and feet to the ground and placed her under the scorching sun. When Asiyah was spending the last moments of her life, her prayer was, «ربّ ابن لى عندك بيتاً فى الجنّة» (39) [My Lord, build for me near You a house in Paradise].

It is not necessary for the role model to be a Prophet or an infallible Imam, nor is it necessary for them to have a history of monotheism. Pharaoh's wife was neither infallible nor a monotheist; rather, she believed upon seeing Moses' miracle.

In introducing role models and honoring personalities, there is no difference between men and women. The Quran commands its Prophet to keep the memory of Abraham alive: «واذكر فى الكتاب ابراهیم» (40) and to remember Maryam: «واذكر فى الكتاب مریم» (41), just as in this verse, He introduces Pharaoh's wife: «ضرب اللّه مثلاً للّذین آمنوا امرائة فرعون».

Characteristics of Pharaoh's Wife 1. She was not influenced by her environment, and society did not shape her personality. She stood against a corrupt regime with logic. 2. She was not dazzled by glamour; she turned her back on all worldly manifestations, abandoning the palace, luxury, and materialism. 3. She broke the atmosphere of silence and repression. 4. She was patient; she died under torture and achieved martyrdom, but she did not abandon her path and school of thought. 5. She was a supporter of the infallible leader of her time, Prophet Moses. 6. She was highly ambitious. She was not satisfied with anything less than Paradise and proximity to Allah. 7. She had courage, and Pharaoh's threats did not affect her. 8. She prioritized the satisfaction of the Creator over the satisfaction of creation. 9. She preferred logic, intellect, and revelation over family matters. 10. Through her forbidding of evil, when she said: «لاتقتلوه» (42), she saved Moses from death in his childhood.

Pharaoh's wife proved that many slogans that have become part of the people's culture over time are wrong and must be changed. They say: If you do not want to be disgraced, conform to the crowd (literally: become the same color as the crowd). But she said: I will not conform to the crowd, nor will I be disgraced. They say: One hand cannot clap (literally: make a sound), but she proved that even one person can create a wave. They say: A woman does not have an independent personality, but she proved that a woman is independent in choosing her path. They say: You cannot fight a banner (or spear) with a fist, but she proved that truth endures, and even if a fist does not affect iron, it affects public opinion throughout history. They say: One flower does not make spring, but she proved that if Allah wills, spring can be made with one flower. They say: So-and-so ate salt and became indebted (to the host). Pharaoh's wife ate Pharaoh's bread and salt, but she detested his belief and said: «نجّنى من فرعون» (My God! Save me from Pharaoh).

Types of Families in the Quran 1. A husband and wife who are like-minded and cooperative in good deeds (such as Imam Ali and Lady Fatimah علیهما السلام). «یطعمون الطعام» (43) 2. A husband and wife who are like-minded and cooperative in wickedness (Abu Lahab and his wife). «تبت یدا ابى لهب... وامرأته حمّالة الحطب» 3. A family where the husband is good, but the wife is bad (the wives of Lot and Noah). «كانتا تحت عبدین... فخانتاهما» (44) 4. A family where the husband is bad, but the wife is good (Pharaoh's wife). «مثلاً للّذین آمنوا امرأة فرعون»

39) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 40) Maryam, 41. 41) Maryam, 16. 42) Al-Qasas, 9. 43) Al-Insan, 8. 44) At-Tahrim, 10.

Messages

  1. A woman can become a role model for the men of history. «ضرب اللّه مثلاً للّذین آمنوا امرأة فرعون»
  2. Comparing role models is one of the methods of education. «مثلاً للّذین كفروا... مثلاً للّذین آمنوا»
  3. Environmental and societal pressure, economic dependence, being a woman, the demands of a spouse, and fear of displacement are none of them reasons for irreligion. «ضرب اللّه مثلاً للّذین آمنوا امرائة فرعون...»
  4. One example is sufficient for completing the argument (Hujjah). «ضرب اللّه مثلاً... امرائة فرعون»
  5. Human will, not anything else, has the final say. «ضرب اللّه مثلاً... امرائة فرعون»
  6. Struggling against the tyrant (Taghut) does not contradict prayer. «ربّ... نجّنى من فرعون»
  7. Salvation from the tyrant requires spiritual support. «ربّ.. نجّنى من فرعون»
  8. More important than Paradise is spiritual proximity to Allah. «ربّ ابن لى عندك...»
  9. Faith causes detestation of oppressors. Pharaoh's wife, due to her faith, detested all of Pharaoh's actions. «نجّنى من فرعون و عمله»
  10. A woman's obedience to men is not required in matters of belief; rather, sometimes she must take a stand. «نجّنى من فرعون»
  11. Family relationships have boundaries, and as soon as they reach deviation and disbelief, they must change. «نجّنى من فرعون»
  12. Disassociation (Tabarri) from disbelief is a condition of faith. «نجّنى من فرعون و عمله»
  13. Expressing detestation and aversion toward others must have a rational and religious standard and criterion. «من فرعون... من القوم الظالمین»

Ayah

وَمَرْيَمَ ٱبْنَتَ عِمْرَٰنَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَحْصَنَتْ فَرْجَهَا فَنَفَخْنَا فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِنَا وَصَدَّقَتْ بِكَلِمَـٰتِ رَبِّهَا وَكُتُبِهِۦ وَكَانَتْ مِنَ ٱلْقَـٰنِتِينَ (۱۲)
And Mary, daughter of Imran, who guarded the chastity of her womb, so We breathed into it of Our spirit. She confirmed the words of her Lord and His Books, and she was one of the obedient. (12)

Key Points

«أحصنت» (Ahsanat) is derived from «حصن» (Hisn), meaning fortress. When used regarding women, it symbolizes chastity and purity. In this Ayah, four perfections are mentioned for Lady Maryam (Mary): chastity, the breathing of the Spirit (Ruh), affirming the Prophets and the heavenly Books, and absolute obedience to Allah. It is stated in both Shi'a and Sunni exegeses (Tafasir) that the four perfect women are: Asiyah, the wife of Pharaoh; Maryam, daughter of Imran; Khadijah, daughter of Khuwaylid; and Fatimah, daughter of the Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله.(45) The only woman whose name is mentioned in the Quran is Lady Maryam. She is mentioned 31 times across twelve Surahs, 12 of which are in the form of 'Isa ibn Maryam (Jesus son of Mary), and one Surah of the Quran is named after Her. Role models must be diverse, reflecting various conditions and environments, so that they can be applicable to everyone, every time, and under different circumstances: the wife of Pharaoh, Lady Maryam, Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham), and Prophet Muhammad علیهم السلام. Allah defends the pure. When they accused Lady Maryam of impurity, «و قولهم على مریم بهتاناً عظیما» (46), Allah stated twice in the Quran that She is chaste: «احصنت فرجها» (47)

45) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 46) Nisa', 156. 47) Anbiya', 91; Tahrim, 12.

Messages

1- The Quran introduces Lady Maryam as a model of chastity for both men and women. «للّذین آمنوا... مریم ابنت عمران»

2- The worth of a woman lies in her chastity. «مریم بنت عمران الّتى احصنت فرجها»

3- Man must take the first step; only then do divine favors pour forth. «احصنت فرجها فنفخنا فیه من روحنا»

4- A pure child arises from a pure lap/womb. «احصنت فرجها فنفخنا فیه من روحنا»

5- Acting upon divine decrees and commands is the best affirmation of them. «صدقت بكلمات ربّها و كتبه»

6- Humility and reverence (Khushu') in the worship of Allah are necessary alongside verbal affirmation. «صدقت... و كانت من القانتین»
